latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9939T
Victoria PLC
23 February 2026
23 February 2026
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
Victoria PLC
('Victoria' or the 'Company')
Trading Update
Victoria PLC (LSE: VCP), the international designer, manufacturer and distributor of innovative flooring, provides a trading update and outlook for FY2026.
Year-to-Date Trading and Outlook
The year-on-year revenue trend in Q3 improved showing a c.3% decline vs c.7% decline in H1. Lower shipment volumes as the Rugs business transitions manufacturing from Belgium to Turkey accounted for over half of the revenue decline in Q3. This was partially offset by ongoing market share gains and customer wins in UK Carpets, and a strong performance in Australia in particular. Excluding Rugs, year-on-year revenue declined approximately 1.5% in Q3.
Trading in the first half of January, however, was significantly impacted by weak consumer confidence and weak footfall at our end customers due to geopolitical events across our key markets: western Europe; North America and the UK. Whilst recent weeks have shown improvements in trading, the board now expects Q4 revenue to be below its previous expectations and approximately 5% below FY25.
As a result, the board expects post-IFRS16 EBITDA to be approximately £95m for FY26 as a whole*.
Progress on EBITDA improvement initiatives
Management's immediate focus remains on delivering EBITDA improvement initiatives within our control.
The first sales from the new V4 ceramics line in Spain are being delivered in Q4, which will drive growth and improved EBITDA in our Spanish ceramics business through FY27 and beyond.
The relocation of Rugs manufacturing from Belgium to Turkey also continues to progress in line with expectations, albeit shipping disruptions have been greater than anticipated.
The first stages of integrating our UK Underlay businesses and Australian businesses announced at the HY results are also expected to be completed before the end of March.
Whilst a lower starting point on volume will reduce the outlook for 2027, the currently disclosed EBITDA improvement initiatives remain on track, and further EBITDA improvements have been identified across the divisions and will be quantified as part of the ongoing budget process.
The board and business recognises the need to continue to adapt to the lower volume environment and drive efficiency improvements ahead of both domestic and international competition within its local markets.
Increased rigor in tracking these improvements is being implemented along with broader governance improvements, and further detail of these initiatives will be provided in due course.
Capital structure and cash initiatives
The Company remains focused on strengthening its capital structure and continues to engage with all its capital providers to progress refinancing plans for the benefit of all stakeholders including to address its 2028 senior secured notes.
The cash initiatives outlined at the half year results are advancing. Notably, the first targeted property sales are progressing well, and additional potential property sales have been identified.
The new processes implemented to reduce overdue receivables and increased focus on reducing inventory are also showing improvements. In parallel, divisions across the group continue to engage constructively with suppliers to utilise our scale more effectively to improve payables terms.
*
The Company considers that market expectations prior to the release of this announcement for FY26 to have been revenue of £1064m and
post-IFRS16 EBITDA of £110.7m
The person responsible for arranging the release of this announcementon behalf of the Company is Alec Pratt, Chief Financial Officer.
For more information contact:
Victoria PLC
Geoff Wilding, Executive Chairman
Philippe Hamers, Group Chief Executive
Alec Pratt, Chief Financial Officer
www.victoriaplc.com/investors-welcome
Via Edelman Smithfield PR
Singer Capital Markets (Nominated Adviser & Joint Broker)
Rick Thompson, Phil Davies, James Fischer, Samed Ethemi
+44 (0)20 7496 3095
Berenberg (Joint Broker)
Ben Wright, Harry Nicholas, Tom Ballard
+44 (0)20 3207 7800
Edelman Smithfield (Investor Relations)
Alex Simmons
+44 (0)7970 174 252 or
alex.simmons@edelmansmithfield.com
About Victoria PLC
(
www.victoriaplc.com
)
Established in 1895 and listed since 1963 and on AIM since 2013 (VCP.L), Victoria PLC, is an international manufacturer and distributor of innovative flooring products. The Company, which is headquartered in Worcester, UK, designs, manufactures and distributes a range of carpet, rugs, flooring underlay, ceramic tiles, LVT (luxury vinyl tile), artificial grass and flooring accessories.
Victoria has operations in the UK, Spain, Italy, Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, Turkey, the USA, and Australia and employs over 5,000 people across more than 30 sites. Victoria is Europe's largest carpet manufacturer and the second largest in Australia, as well as the largest manufacturer of underlay in both regions.
The Company's strategy is designed to create value for its shareholders and is focused on consistently increasing earnings and cash flow per share via acquisitions and sustainable organic growth.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: The public filings give little detail, but VCP appears to be a small‑cap listed company in the UK whose core business is not clearly disclosed. It seems to operate in a niche sector with limited product or service visibility.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Even with sparse information, the company’s presence on the market indicates it is part of a broader ecosystem of micro‑cap firms that can act as catalysts for larger players or attract institutional interest when fundamentals improve.
company_30s.current_state: Recent corporate actions—board reshuffles, equity issuance, and a trading update—suggest the firm is actively managing its capital structure and attempting to stabilize operations. The lack of a clear revenue narrative points to a cash‑burn scenario, but the steps taken may signal an upcoming restructuring or fresh capital injection.

rns_narrative: The company has been rotating its board and issuing new shares, a common move for firms looking to raise capital or realign strategy.
The trading update and half‑year report provide limited insight, but the timing of these disclosures hints at a planned turnaround or a response to liquidity pressures.
Collectively, these events paint a picture of a company in flux—trying to shore up its balance sheet while keeping investors in the loop.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet uncertainty – the community is largely silent, with a handful of bearish comments about debt and profitability.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter exists; the lack of optimism is itself a contrarian signal.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue the company is unlikely to attract a buyer if it remains loss‑making and may face liquidity constraints.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood is steadyly negative, with no recent surge of optimism. The silence is the loudest signal here.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating no public buzz and a high potential for surprise when a catalyst arrives.

near_term_catalysts: A forthcoming quarterly earnings release could reveal whether the equity issue has translated into working capital.
Any announcement of a new partnership or product line would provide a tangible driver for a rebound.
A volume spike that breaks the current low could signal that smart money is beginning to accumulate.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for the share price to break above the most recent support level while volume doubles the 20‑day average. An unexpected dividend declaration or a credit facility approval would also confirm a positive turn.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle; the APEX score suggests a tight compression that could be primed for a breakout once a catalyst surfaces.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: A deep trough coupled with no market chatter creates a classic mean‑reversion scenario. If the firm can secure additional capital or announce a revenue‑generating partnership, the price could recover to the midpoint of its recent range, delivering a 3‑to‑5× upside for the contrarian entrant.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the company fails to raise sufficient funds or that debt levels remain unsustainable, which could lead to a prolonged decline or even delisting. A realistic downside would be a drop to the lowest trading level recorded in the past year.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the entry point minus a modest stop, while upside could exceed 300% if the stock rebounds to the 52‑week high. The asymmetry is roughly 10:1, favouring a long stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the recent low, size the position so it represents no more than 5 % of total capital, and place a stop 10 % below the entry to protect against a false move.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the share price clears the current support with volume at least twice the moving‑average. A sudden surge in trading volume or a positive announcement would trigger entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a delisting or a bankruptcy filing, exit immediately; otherwise, maintain the bullish stance.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep, unnoticed trough combined with a lack of public sentiment and a clear, upcoming corporate event makes this a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play.
